steps.title: Identify the structure around the blank
steps.content:
Left side: the noun "Police" (part of the noun phrase "the International Criminal Police").
Right side: the prepositional phrase "in 1923".

steps.title: Complete the matching em dash pair
steps.content:
Rule: when an em dash opens a nonrestrictive appositive mid-sentence, a second em dash must close it. The sentence reads: "Belgium joined Interpol—the International Criminal Police Organization_ in 1923." The blank is the closing punctuation.

A: "...Police Organization— in 1923..." — the em dash closes the nonrestrictive appositive, matching the opening em dash after "Interpol." Correct.

B: "...Police Organization, in 1923..." — a comma doesn't match the opening em dash; mismatched pair leaves the appositive improperly closed. Incorrect.

C: "...Police Organization in 1923..." — no closing punctuation; the appositive runs unclosed into the rest of the sentence. Incorrect.

D: "...Police Organization: in 1923..." — a colon introduces a list or elaboration and cannot close an em-dash-opened phrase. Incorrect.
